title: eryngium constancei
intro: Eryngium constancei, also known as Constance's eryngium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, commonly referred to as the sea holly. This perennial plant is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, and it is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves and flowers of the Constance's eryngium have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall health and well-being. The plant can be prepared in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
